titleStmt.title: Saudi Arabia 2022 – Speaker 14 – Houses; Education
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Saudi Arabia Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: Hopefully you like my coffee
Yes, it is Turkish, right?
it's black ((interesting because usually it is sōda according to reem (maybe observers paradox))
go ahead, take this it is sweet
go ahead, but I recommend this one ((not sure if _́rašši-h or _́raššiḥ?))
and this is the water
When they started to move and taking leases here, everyone came and started fixing up their houses,((the ending ōn is weird))
Do you see how the houses were in the beginning? I will show you with a pen. ((her kānit is also strange (usually it would be kēnit) kānit will a full open ā is kuwaiti))
Did they tell you about how the houses were before?
No, they told me about tents but they did not tell me about the houses.
Yes.
Yes, I had forgotten, but surely you saw it on the internet.
These are all the words
Thank you very much
May God grant you good health, you see
the houses before, they left the tents
Afterwards they came here and built their houses, meaning the people who developed, they were the first to use stones, you know this thing.
They built houses with stones, afterwards the people who began to sedentarize as well, not like this
they would build houses, rooms, a room like this, a room like this
yes
and here there is an open courtyard, meaning you can look at the sky
yes, their houses were like this
yes but...
not...
around it there are rooms
yes
all of it
an enclosure
but half
an enclosure (for animals) and rooms
our house was like this
our first home was like this
yes, and naturally, the washing machine, they would wash their clothes outside and the washing machine would be outside, the houses were designed as such
the washing machine would be outside
and the storages
the water tank and things like that
everything in the enclosure or actually outside?
No outside, in the enclosure we would only, meaning it would rain and pour down
When they started to develop, no, it was over. They started to, all those who had their houses like this they started to do what? We call it ġamōha (roof top)
You see? We call it a ġamōh , meaning they would cover
ġamōha?
ġamōha, like this, it was like this, rooms, they would build rooms (the speaker is drawing the shap of a ġamōha), that's it, like this
All of this was open
it was simple, then it developed and with this development what happened? They began to use the ġamōha
meaning they covered it
With what? Was it like a normal roof?
No, it was wood, it wasn't metal
houses, we call them public houses
wood, wide wood like this
and then there was another type of wood, black ash
they called it black ash
Did it come from the palm tree or?
No, it was manufactured
It was manufactured, now they use metal
Look, public houses
Take this, I recommend the sweet one, this one
yes the one with chocolate
public tents, let's call them public houses

It was wood, I don't know how to explain it to you, like this do you see? ((showing her a drawing or a photo)), like this do you see, but we are a Najdi family ((what does she mean by this??)) look this is the wood that I'm talking about ((hiki is weird))
It was like this, but they did not build pillars like this for us
No most its shape was copied from the Najdi houses
And afterwards, that's it, they developed and what did they become?
They covered them with wood, this is the wood, do you see?
Naturally you saw...
Naturally
It was wood and not metal, do you understand?
Did they bring sheep with them or not?
Yes, every person brought his sheep with him, his sheep in his section, we still have...
There were fields
Yes, there were fields
Naturally, in the beginning they would come and section the land
That's it, this part for that person, this one for the other, that one for that man, that's it, in the beginning our system of ownership was like this
The people would determine the boundaries of the land and take it
Now, in these years this is not possible, the situation changed
It is necessary to have names (presented) and documents and I don't know what
Documents meaning, legal ones which have your name and your country ((dōla is strange))
meaning, now that's it
The Rashayda became like this, some of them, I don't know much about these periods, you know? But I can tell you what I saw
And afterwards, they started to educate their daughters
Look I will tell you
Before, the girls would not go to school
No, no, no they would not go to school
But myself and my generation we were raised and we all were educated
Do you know?
From a young age
Yes, from a young age, no, one (speaking to someone else momentarily)
Yes, from our childhood we were raised like this, because in the beginning we weren't here originally
We were in this city, in Taif, with our father who was a soldier
Do you see? The first school here was the first school in aṯ-Ṯimid, in all of this region
It was established in the year 1399 (h.) (1978 C.E)
How long ago is that (in common era)?
Meaning around 43 years ago
Not so long ago then
Yes, not so long ago that it was established, do you understand?
They studied up until the three years of middle school, do you know about the middle school?
Three years of middle school, they would go to an institute
It wasn't a Bachelor's
Most of them went on to do a Bachelor's
In the institute, most of them
There weren't any Saudi Arabian teachers, there weren't
finally
Who would teach you?
Egyptians, Syrians, there were utterly no Saudi Arabian teachers
The first Saudi Arabian teacher, I remember she came from Khaybar
I was... In the sixth year of elementary school
Look, how long ago was this?
How long ago was this?
Disclose your age
Ok good, I will tell you how old I was, if for example it was around the 6th year, what is the age in the sixth year? Twelve years old, in the sixth year
In the sixth year I would have been twelve years, now I am fourty two, fourty three, something like that ((b- may be an abrev. of beyn))
That is when the first female teacher came from Khaybar.
Afterwards, the girls in aṯ-Ṯimid here, the girls of the Rashayda
I did not know them well, but they were limited
Up to know, they haven't retired
I know them, here in Saudi Arabia they retire after thirty years.
30 years of labour, work, do you understand me?
Up to now they haven't retired, they were around five.
After that, you my sisters and I we were always at the top of our grade, my sisters were even better, they were four, three years older, the ones that you saw there.
My sisters that were there they all graduated from university
Before me, them and their class
Even if they were limited. And people would not, only those who had the means would educate their daughters,((ʕind-am - ʕinduhum))
They would go to highschool
In contrast to my (scholastic) beginning, they would go to highschool
We call it 'they take on highschool'
We say 'they take on highschool', meaning they study in highschool
Look how we use the word 'to take'
From 'taking' like so, 'he takes something'
We say 'they take on highschool'
Meaning they get a certificate from highschool
They graduate highschool, most of them remained at home
Because the departments, the Bachelor studies, higher education, they were in the city
150 km away
Far away
Yes, and those 5 teachers, approximately, they were from aḏ-Ḏhiba, aḏ-Ḏhiba, and then the generation of my sisters commenced (who were pursuing higher education).
Three, four, five, a lot, ten, this means they were a lot
They went to the city, then after them, how much time passed? Up until my generation, you see
Not every class managed to go to the universities
The institutes were under a great deal of stress, the institutes that we have here, Khaybar, that came ((not sure about the translation of twaǧǧaʕ))
Which institutes?
An institute, they would be teaching the young primary students, teachers for the young primary students.
They would not teach middle school and highschool.
In the institutes we have the primary lessons, only the primary grade
Most of my generation went to these institutes
and the generation after me as well
The woman was allowed to practise this profession
Yes as a teacher, originally she wouldn't work, meaning that they would barely allow her to be a teacher
Even the nurses who we have here, the nurses and the female doctors, possibly, the perspective (on them) changed in the period following our generation
It became possible for them to become a nurse, it was possible...
Yes, but until the information...
Get up, take this, close the door
Amal, lock the door
I don't want to, I don't want to
My love, ??
I don't want to sit here
